transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# maw arm crib (current v2 `ws/` layout)

You coordinate multi-agent work using **maw**, a workspace-management tool built on git. The repo is laid out as a bare-style root containing `.git/` and a `ws/` directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `ws/<name>/`. The default integration target lives at `ws/default/`. The `maw` binary is on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` — make a new workspace (worktree) at `ws/<name>/`.
- `maw ws list` — list all workspaces with their state (active / stale / conflicted).
- `maw ws sync` — bring the current workspace up to the latest epoch (rebases workspace commits onto integration head). Use when you see `stale` in `maw ws list`.
- `maw ws diff <name>` — show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --check` — dry-run a merge; reports conflicts without changing state.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --destroy` — merge workspace(s) into default and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` — drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).
- `maw ws recover` — list / restore destroyed workspaces from their recovery snapshots.
- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` — resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.
- `maw doctor` — substrate-health probe.

## Running commands inside a workspace

The sandbox does not persist `cd` between tool calls. Use `maw exec <workspace> -- <cmd>` to run any command inside a workspace:

```
maw exec alice -- git status
maw exec alice -- git add -A
maw exec alice -- git commit -m "feat: …"
```

## Conflicts are data, not errors

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict — it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.
- `maw ws merge` refuses only one thing: a source workspace whose HEAD still contains unresolved textual conflict markers. Pass `--force` only when markers are legitimate content (test fixtures, docs).

## Prime Invariant

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `maw --help` and `maw ws --help` for the full surface.
- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmpBNKedA
Scenario seed: 7991878095955336068

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. In workspace `ws-0`, edit the following files (any content is fine; the test cares about coordination): shared/file-3.txt
3. Commit pending changes in workspace `ws-0` with a descriptive message.
4. Remove workspace `ws-0`.
transcript.convention_text:
# maw arm crib (current v2 `ws/` layout)

You coordinate multi-agent work using **maw**, a workspace-management tool built on git. The repo is laid out as a bare-style root containing `.git/` and a `ws/` directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `ws/<name>/`. The default integration target lives at `ws/default/`. The `maw` binary is on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` — make a new workspace (worktree) at `ws/<name>/`.
- `maw ws list` — list all workspaces with their state (active / stale / conflicted).
- `maw ws sync` — bring the current workspace up to the latest epoch (rebases workspace commits onto integration head). Use when you see `stale` in `maw ws list`.
- `maw ws diff <name>` — show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --check` — dry-run a merge; reports conflicts without changing state.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --destroy` — merge workspace(s) into default and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` — drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).
- `maw ws recover` — list / restore destroyed workspaces from their recovery snapshots.
- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` — resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.
- `maw doctor` — substrate-health probe.

## Running commands inside a workspace

The sandbox does not persist `cd` between tool calls. Use `maw exec <workspace> -- <cmd>` to run any command inside a workspace:

```
maw exec alice -- git status
maw exec alice -- git add -A
maw exec alice -- git commit -m "feat: …"
```

## Conflicts are data, not errors

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict — it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.
- `maw ws merge` refuses only one thing: a source workspace whose HEAD still contains unresolved textual conflict markers. Pass `--force` only when markers are legitimate content (test fixtures, docs).

## Prime Invariant

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `maw --help` and `maw ws --help` for the full surface.
- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

turns.reply_text: I'll complete this task battery step by step. Let me start by checking the current state and then executing the tasks.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws list","description":"Check current workspace state"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: default	/tmp/.tmpBNKedA/ws/default

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws create ws-0","description":"Create workspace ws-0"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: Workspace create requires an explicit source.
  Use one of:
    maw ws create --from main ws-0
    maw ws create --change <change-id> ws-0

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws create --from main ws-0","description":"Create workspace ws-0 from main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Creating workspace 'ws-0' at ws/ws-0 ...

Workspace 'ws-0' ready!

  Mode:   ephemeral
  Epoch:  df345635ff39 (base commit for this workspace)
  Path:   /tmp/.tmpBNKedA/ws/ws-0/

  IMPORTANT: All file reads, writes, and edits must use this path.
  This is your working directory for ALL operations, not just bash.

To start working:

  # Edit files under /tmp/.tmpBNKedA/ws/ws-0/
  # Changes are detected automatically by the merge engine

  # Run commands in the workspace:
  maw exec ws-0 -- cargo test
  maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy

Note: All edits in the workspace are tracked automatically.
The merge engine captures changes when merging.

tool_calls.name: Read
tool_calls.args_json: {"file_path":"/tmp/.tmpBNKedA/ws/ws-0/shared/file-3.txt"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: File does not exist. Note: your current working directory is /tmp/.tmpBNKedA.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- find . -type f | head -20","description":"List files in the workspace"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
./README.md
./.git

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- ls -la","description":"List root directory of workspace"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
total 8
drwxr-xr-x 2 bob bob 80 May 28 01:06 .
drwxr-xr-x 4 bob bob 80 May 28 01:06 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 bob bob 48 May 28 01:06 .git
-rw-r--r-- 1 bob bob 11 May 28 01:06 README.md

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.args_json: {"content":"Edited content for file-3.txt in workspace ws-0.\n","file_path":"/tmp/.tmpBNKedA/ws/ws-0/shared/file-3.txt"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmpBNKedA/ws/ws-0/shared/file-3.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git add -A && git commit -m \"feat: edit shared/file-3.txt in workspace ws-0\"","description":"Commit changes in workspace ws-0"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 128
fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- cat .git","description":"Check .git file content"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: gitdir: /tmp/.tmpBNKedA/repo.git/worktrees/ws-0

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git status","description":"Check git status in workspace"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Not currently on any branch.
Changes to be committed:
  (use "git restore --staged <file>..." to unstage)
	new file:   shared/file-3.txt

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git log --oneline -5","description":"Check git log"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: df34563 init

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git branch -a","description":"List branches in workspace"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
* (no branch)
+ main

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git rev-parse HEAD","description":"Check HEAD commit"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: df345635ff3971dedb9ba6c51fdfdb728f4076d2

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git commit -m \"feat: edit shared/file-3.txt in workspace ws-0\"","description":"Commit changes in workspace ws-0"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
[detached HEAD 039dba1] feat: edit shared/file-3.txt in workspace ws-0
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)
 create mode 100644 shared/file-3.txt

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws destroy ws-0","description":"Destroy workspace ws-0"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: Workspace 'ws-0' has 1 unmerged change(s) (state: committed-unintegrated). Refusing destroy to avoid data loss.
  Recommended: maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy
  Preview options: maw ws destroy ws-0 --dry-run --format json   (bn-29fi)
  Or force-destroy: maw ws destroy ws-0 --force
    (--force captures a recovery snapshot first (Prime Invariant: no committed work is ever lost). Recover with: maw ws recover ws-0)
  Inspect first: maw ws touched ws-0 --format json

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy","description":"Merge workspace ws-0 into default and destroy"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: No --message provided and stdin is not a terminal.
  Usage: maw ws merge <workspaces> --into <target> --message "feat: description of changes"
  
  A commit message is required so that merge history captures intent.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy --message \"feat: integrate ws-0 changes to shared/file-3.txt\"","description":"Merge workspace ws-0 into default and destroy with message"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Adopting workspace: ws-0

PREPARE: Freezing merge inputs...
  Epoch: df345635ff39
  ws-0: 039dba15d306

BUILD: Running merge engine...
  1 unique path(s), 0 shared path(s), 1 resolved
  Candidate: 5bcc93bd2d98


VALIDATE: No validation commands configured — skipping.

COMMIT: Advancing epoch...
  Epoch advanced: df345635ff39 → 5bcc93bd2d98
  Branch 'main' updated.

AUTO-REBASE: replaying sibling workspaces onto new epoch...
  ws-0 — skipped: in progress

CLEANUP...
  Default workspace updated to new epoch.
  Cleaning up workspaces...
    Destroyed: ws-0 (snapshot saved → maw ws recover ws-0)

Merged to main: feat: integrate ws-0 changes to shared/file-3.txt from ws-0

Next: push to remote:
  maw push

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws list","description":"Verify workspace list"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: default	/tmp/.tmpBNKedA/ws/default

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec default -- git log --oneline -5","description":"Check git log in default workspace"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
5bcc93b feat: integrate ws-0 changes to shared/file-3.txt
df34563 init

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec default -- cat shared/file-3.txt","description":"Verify file content in default workspace"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: Edited content for file-3.txt in workspace ws-0.

turns.reply_text:
Done. All tasks completed successfully:

1. ✓ Created workspace `ws-0` from the main branch
2. ✓ Edited `shared/file-3.txt` in the workspace
3. ✓ Committed changes with message "feat: edit shared/file-3.txt in workspace ws-0"
4. ✓ Removed workspace `ws-0` (merged into default and destroyed)

The substrate is coherent — changes were integrated into the default workspace before destroying ws-0, maintaining the Prime Invariant with no lost work.
